w:r.w:t: The goal of this test is to develop a responsive web application that integrates with the
) to provide real-time and historical weather insights. The application must automatically detect the user's location via browser GPS upon landing to display localized data.
The application must load and render all data within
The UI and all data visualizations must be fully mobile-friendly.
This page should display information for the current date by default or a specific date selected via a
1. Individual Weather Variables: Display the following parameters clearly as individual values:
Precipitation , Relative Humidity , and UV Index.
Maximum Wind Speed and Precipitation Probability Max.
Air Quality Index , PM10 , PM2.5 , Carbon Monoxide (CO) , Carbon Dioxide (CO2) , Nitrogen Dioxide (NO2) , and Sulphur Dioxide (SO2).
2. Hourly Data Visualizations: Create individual graphs for each bullet point below, plotted at hourly intervals for the selected date:
Temperature. (User can toggle from centigrade to fahrenheit)
PM10 & PM2.5 (Combined in a single graph).
A separate navigation view where users can analyze long-term trends by selecting a date range (up to a maximum of 2 years).
Historical Charts: The developer must choose the most suitable chart type (line, bar, etc.) based on the nature of the variables.
Max Wind Speed and Dominant Wind Direction.
All graphs and charts must include the following interactive features:
Ability to zoom in and out of specific timeframes.
Components must automatically adjust to remain legible on mobile screens.
Submit all the items mentioned below through this
Correct integration with the Open-Meteo API and browser GPS.
Effective use of charts and responsive design principles.
You have to submit the assignment within
of receiving it. We expect that the assignment will take you 8 hours to complete, so please schedule your time accordingly . Late submissions will not be accepted.
You will be informed of the results of your selection test within 3 working days of submitting your assignment, via email.

If you pass the selection test, you will be invited for the final step — the personal interview. You will have multiple slots to choose from, on Mondays and Thursday. Interviews will last 30-45 minutes
